content: My schedule is so unpredictable with the ER shifts. I never know if I'll be working days or nights.
That sounds challenging. How do you manage your sleep and personal time with such varying hours?
It's tough. I try to keep a consistent bedtime when I'm on day shift - usually in bed by 10:30 since I have to be up at 5:30 for my 7:00 AM start at City Hospital.
And when you're on night shift?
I work 7 PM to 7 AM, so I try to sleep from 8:30 AM to 3:30 PM. But my upstairs neighbor has a toddler who runs around during the day, so I wear earplugs and use blackout curtains.
That sounds exhausting. Do you have any routines that help you transition between shifts?
I've started a small ritual - when I get home from night shift, I sit on my balcony for 15 minutes with a cup of chamomile tea before going to bed. It helps me decompress.
That's a great idea. What about your days off? How do you adjust back to a more normal schedule?

I usually stay up until around midnight on my first day off, then sleep in until 9. By the second day, I'm back to a more regular schedule. I use that time to work on my YouTube channel - I'm teaching people basic piano.
That's impressive! How long have you been doing the YouTube channel?
I just started it last month. I've always loved playing piano, and I figured since I'm up at odd hours anyway, I might as well be productive. I film the videos on my days off.
Do you have any other hobbies or activities that help you unwind after stressful shifts?
I go salsa dancing every Wednesday night when I'm not working. It's at this club downtown - I've been going for about two years now. The live music and movement really help me release stress.
